slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) documents a completed Q1 2024 vendor-control review with a focus on closing a control by verifying missing evidence, while the Privileged Account Approval Email outlines a conditional, time-bound access approval requiring quarterly recertification and vendor confirmation for ongoing compliance. The former is a synthetic artifact for testing evidence completeness, whereas the latter is a synthetic template for a recurring, review-dependent approval process.

- Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) is a synthetic artifact for testing evidence completeness in a completed Q1 2024 review.  
- Privileged Account Approval Email is a synthetic template for a conditional, time-bound access approval requiring quarterly recertification.  
- Evidence Log closure depends on verifying missing evidence; Approval Email hinges on vendor confirmation before each quarter.  
- Evidence Log is tied to a specific completed review; Approval Email is tied to ongoing, quarterly conditional approvals.  
- Evidence Log involves archival of an email-export artifact; Approval Email mandates vendor confirmation of personnel before each quarter.  
- Both documents are explicitly synthetic and intended solely for testing purposes.
